# ipzilon_landing_zone (Resource)

Manages a landing zone inside a hub. Supports parent/child hierarchy (max 2 levels).

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Root landing zone
resource "ipzilon_landing_zone" "root" {
  hub_id      = ipzilon_hub.example.id
  name        = "shared-services"
  description = "Shared Services Landing Zone"
}

# Child landing zone
resource "ipzilon_landing_zone" "child" {
  hub_id    = ipzilon_hub.example.id
  parent_id = ipzilon_landing_zone.root.id
  name      = "app-tier"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `hub_id` (Number) Hub this landing zone belongs to.
- `name` (String) Resource name (must be lowercase — the server normalizes all strings).

### Optional

- `cidr` (String) Optional CIDR assigned to this landing zone.
- `description` (String) Free-text description.
- `parent_id` (Number) Parent landing zone ID (omit for root-level LZ).

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import ipzilon_landing_zone.example 1
```
